Tests if dividing `Number.NaN` by `Number.MAX_VALUE` results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of dividing `Number.NaN` by `Number.MAX_VALUE` in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of this division is `NaN`, which is expected to be true. The test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles arithmetic operations involving `NaN`, which is a special numeric value representing "Not-a-Number." This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's number handling.
